author: @Arujin
text: Great video. I'm planning on doing the same, and this is a simple and effective solution. I wonder why they don't make office chairs as confortable as car seats, especially older car seats. Nice video to the point and clear.

author: @R3TR0--93
text: now you just gotta install adjustable arm rests

author: @stewartrv
text: Of course now winters here, having heated car seats could be a great way to go! 😄

author: @dillanmistry
text: I done the same alike thing except without the hydraulics because it was too high so I made my own frame

author: @peterwillis9864
text: Just what I need. Cheers

author: @carolynm6896
text: I've wanted to do this for years.

author: @richardsandwell2285
text:
This is fantastic, it is a great reuse of a valuable resource and I can imagine car fanatics who are attached to their vehicles and end up for whatever reason scrapping their beloved, being able to reuse their car chairs for sentimental reasons. 
This is great from an environmental stand point.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: I agree, I wish my mum never scrapped her land rover, the seats were awesome, with heated and massage functions. That would have been awesome! Thanks mate! :)

author: @ambersmith6517
text: good video

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Thank you! :)

author: @stevem6259
text: Brilliant!

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Cheers mate! :)

author: @ZoVoSinc
text: Always been something I wanted to do, my ford probe seats are glorious. This video was great, Luke you're really smashing em out of the park, keep the vids coming!

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Thank you very much mate! :)

author: @Twistedmetal-qe8kx
text: Awesome, time to go seat hunting at the auto wreckers. Nothing like a  comfortable work chair!

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Oh I agree big time! If my chair is uncomfortable it takes me much longer to zone into whatever I'm doing. Thanks mate! :)

author: @Forbidden303
text: Everyone is well chuffed!

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Hahah agreed, Everyone seems to like this one! cheers mate! :)

author: @sandeepnaik6818
text: Best resuse of car seat. If you don't mind can you make generator from car engine?

author: @MrTubeuser12
text: wow, there's something I never thought of doing, nicely done. I tell you what if you can score some seats from a luxury car or 4wd you'd have an amazing seat, PC gamers would love to have something like this, they pay a fortune for "gaming chairs" that most of the time are honestly not comfortable at all. I think you might be onto something here.

author: @FoodwaysDistribution
text: The most obvious conversion is to an ejector seat 😁

author: @neatt3815
text: Might have to do this one day. One if the great things about this is that most passenger seats are in perfect condition and a lot cheaper compared to a driver's seat.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: That is true, The passager side of these seats was much less worn compared to the driver's side. I still took the drivers though it just felt better lol. Thanks mate! :)

author: @TheMan1510
text: This has to be one of the coolest ideas ever! Well done!

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Thank you mate! :)

author: @NorthernKitty
text:
Fantastic!!  I just bought a used 15-passenger van back in January that I'm converting into a camper, and it has 4 bench seats I need to get rid of.  I was joking with a friend about taking 2 of the seats, face them toward each other with a table between and make a dining booth in my kitchen... they'd match the decor! LOL!! 😋

After watching this, maybe I'll get serious about that idea... you both looked awfully comfortable!!  Great job, Luke!!  You're always an inspiration!!  😊

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: I love the idea of converting a van into a camper, I hope it goes really well! The idea of making a dining booth in the kitchen is awesome! They would be super. Thank you! :)

author: @matthewgallagher4913
text: Great video - well done!  The chair needs some arms though.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: I agree, i dont think it would be that hard to attach something though, that would be super. Thanks for the suggestion mate! :)

author: @saifcathum3423
text: I like this. Off to the junk yard for some car seats.

author: @BoB4jjjjs
text:
We had car seats way back, but they were not a comfortable back then,  But it was good enough for a few louts to hang around thinking what trouble we could land ourselves in next!  No headrests in ours, we were real men, well boys actually!!
EDIT:  They didn't swivel round either, they might have done if we had got hold of that type of seat legs.
Nice job though, why waste good seats?

author: @robertpitt8418
text: Perfect  : )

author: @AM-us7cz
text: Luke  Best  video yet  ha ha l love it.  How much is the first Bid on Mr Murrays chair ?

author: @Teknopottu
text: Nice job! Made myself an office chair out of Volvo 240 seat some 10 years ago. Never a sore back with the adjustable couch and if cold, seat warmer can be enabled. Confiest chair I've sat in.

author: @scotttovey
text:
Excellent Idea.
Especially when you consider just how cheap office chair padding has become.

author: @avanciazim1447
text: Awesome seats! What was the type of the car?

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: It was a mk1 Nissan Micra k10. cool car, but some really comfortable seats. Thanks mate! :)

author: @stevetobias4890
text: Great project Luke. I must admit I have sat in some extremely comfortable car seats before (and some not so comfortable). Converting them to desk chairs is one hell of an idea.  Kudos mate 👍

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Thank you mate! I really like how these turned out! :)

author: @TheMaxthesis
text: Now this is something I could actually do myself =) Great video, thanks guys!

author: @roberthopgood1894
text:
You did not explain WHY you had to add the 2nd piece of wood
What exactly is it for/does it do???

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Its meant to help fill the gap between the adaption plate and the chair, it helps spread the load from the stem and the wheel to the chair. Hope this helps mate! :)

author: @dr_ned_flanders
text: I am in the market for a luxury office chair and an electric, heated, massaging BMW or Mercedes chair would be just the ticket.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Yeah a nice BMW seat would do the trick, nice and low, full leather and heated functions. Awesome! Thanks mate! :)

replies.author: @dr_ned_flanders
replies.text: @MyNameIsLukey Thanks to your video I have actually just bought a E90 leather seat for the bargain price of £40. So expect to modify my old office chair soon. It is a great idea for a really awesome chair. Great video.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: @dr_ned_flanders From first-hand experience I know that they are super comfy, the old e46 coupe I had was awesome full leather interior, and omg it was amazing. I do regret selling it. just for the comfort lol. :)

author: @55Ramius
text: My fairly new chair is falling apart. They look great in the store but the super-thin covering is just peeling and splitting like crazy. I am super gentle with it and it still did this. I think a car seat would outlast 50 of these chairs I have been buying. I don't buy the cheapest either but not getting anyway near the value I should. Thanks a ton for the idea!  Wonders how much a Lambo seat cost from the scrapyard??   : )

author: @karlmyers6518
text: A few years ago I tried looking for a high end comfy chair but failed.  This is awesome mate

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: I have been looking at them recently too, but £200+ for a decent "gaming chair" didn't seem worth it to me. so I built my own lol. Cheers mate! :)

replies.author: @karlmyers6518
replies.text: @MyNameIsLukey yes I know what you mean,  I have a comfortable bucket seat ready to build one.

author: @Arachnoid_of_the_underverse
text: It came out good Luke, you could also make a lounger from the back seat.

author: @Gwydion67
text:
Hm, my own office chair is quite old (about 25 years), could use a replacement. Great idea for such.
I already think about adding arm rests ...and maybe even a foot rest, would make it a suitable improvised sleeping furniture (don't dare to call it "bed", lol). 😴😅

Thanks and a doeble thumbs up,  for Rob and you! 👍👍

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: hahaha I mean if it lays flat, that's good enough to sleep on lol! Yeah, that would be pretty awesome to add arm rests and a foot rest too. Thank you mate! :)

author: @HtwoHH
text: BRILLIANT!

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Cheers mate! :)

replies.author: @HtwoHH
replies.text: @MyNameIsLukey soooo many people spending hundreds on gaming chairs. Looks like this is a better product made from someone's trash. My kinda innovation 😁 someone with the right junkyard nearby could make a decent living making these I think

author: @WileHeCoyote
text: GREAT IDEA  LUKE!  currently watching/typing from atop a 2000 Pontiac Firebird seat shoved in the top of a plastic milkcrate! lol CHEERS!

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Hahaha awesome! Improvise, adapt and overcome lol. loving the idea! Cheers mate! :)

author: @Alex-tj1zo
text:
This is such a rewarding and simple prolect !
Thank you so much Luke !
👍😀
PS: Reminds me of the Top Gear's learher couch  😃

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Hahaha awesome! I do love that couch. Cheers mate! :)

author: @ToninFightsEntropy
text: Excellent idea! I've been thinking about adding the headrests to my sofa for a while, though I have a question about the height of this seat.. I'm rather short, and heels need to be on the ground for good ergonomics, does it go low enough? Have you thought of a way to mount it lower down if not? I'm only 5'5 so my heels don't reach the floor from the lowest position on most computer chairs lol and comfort is a never-ending issue for me!

replies.author: @judyofthewoods
replies.text: I have the same problem, even shorter. I made a comfy office chair that was going to make it too high even on the lowest level, but managed to find a shorter than standard gas-lift replacement. They do come in different lengths, and they are not too expensive.

author: @ryanjamesloyd6733
text: oh, Well done!  that is such a good idea.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Thank you mate! :)

author: @The-Green-Manali-shi
text: That's great Luke. Can you make Rob's an ejector seat? I want to hear him shout AWESOME!!!! from 50ft in the air. :-)

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Hahahah that did make me chuckle lol. Thanks mate! :)

author: @brainretardant
text: Keep that seat belt for the pole shift.  Its gonna be a doozie

author: @herbetone
text: I would have never had thought of doing that, great idea Luke, and a thumbs up from the boss.😎

author: @gojohnniegogo
